disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gradual, symmetrical difficulty understanding speech over several years, worse in background noise, with preserved awareness that sound is present
trigger_question_examples: How long has the hearing trouble been going on?
Is it one ear or both, and did it come on suddenly or slowly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been creeping up for years now, both ears the same. He can tell people are talking, he just can't make out the words — and it's hopeless if there's any noise or a few people at once.

trigger_question_examples: Any ringing or noises in the ears?
Is the ringing in one ear or both?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — a constant high ringing in both ears, it never really stops.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No ear pain, no sudden loss or blockage, no recent trauma, no discharge
trigger_question_examples: Any pain in the ears, or a sudden change or blockage?
Any discharge, or a recent knock or injury to the ear or head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pain at all, nothing sudden, no gunk coming out, and no bumps or injuries. It's just slowly got worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute agitation and new suspiciousness tonight that family are deliberately whispering to exclude him, arising from communication frustration
trigger_question_examples: What changed tonight that made you call?
How is his mood and behaviour right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Tonight he got really worked up and convinced we were all whispering about him on purpose. He's normally not like this — it's the not-being-able-to-hear that sets him off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new confusion about time or place, no drowsiness, no fluctuating alertness, no fever — the suspiciousness is confined to the belief people are hiding what they say
trigger_question_examples: Is he muddled about where he is or what day it is, or more drowsy than usual?
Any fever, or is he drifting in and out of being alert?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he knows exactly where he is and what's going on — he's sharp, just furious and certain we're whispering. No fever, not sleepy, nothing like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive social withdrawal, low mood and marital strain from the communication barrier
trigger_question_examples: How has this been affecting him day to day and his mood?
Has he pulled back from people he used to see?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's stopped coming to family things, he gets frustrated and down about it, and honestly it's been really hard on our marriage.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No expressed thoughts of self-harm or wanting to die when sensitively asked
trigger_question_examples: With how low and frustrated he's been, has he ever said he doesn't want to be here anymore?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that, thank goodness — he's frustrated and down but he's never said anything about hurting himself.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: He wouldn't do anything silly... but no, he hasn't said that.
